message:
fix(core,mesh,sandbox): safe integer casts in HRW hashing, fix was_bounded false positive, fix Linux sandbox threading

Three targeted production bug fixes:

1. fcp-core/checkpoint.rs + quorum.rs: Replace unsafe `as u32` casts
   with `u32::try_from(...).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` for length prefixes in
   HRW hash computation and SignatureSet serialization. Prevents silent
   truncation on pathologically long inputs. Golden test vectors and CBOR
   test data regenerated to match the updated hash output.

2. fcp-mesh/symbol_request.rs: Fix `was_bounded` false positive in
   SymbolResponseBuilder::add_from_repair_engine(). The old check
   `available_count >= max_response_symbols` produced false positives
   because select_symbols() already caps at max_response_symbols. Moved
   the computation to build() where total_available is known, using
   `was_bounded = sent_count < remaining`.

3. fcp-sandbox/linux.rs: Fix sandbox syscall policy for threaded runtimes.
   Moved CLONE, CLONE3, WAIT4 from deny_exec=false block to base allowed
   list (threads needed by Rust/Go async runtimes even with exec denied).
   Removed RLIMIT_AS enforcement (modern runtimes reserve large virtual
   address spaces up-front, causing immediate crashes). Disabled
   RLIMIT_NPROC=0 when deny_exec=true because on Linux NPTL, NPROC
   limits both processes AND threads, killing Tokio worker threads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
